title: Teacher — Gradebook
content:
Overview


The Gradebook is where teachers enter and manage student grades for each subject. Grades are organized by term and year, and teachers can add comments visible to students and parents. The gradebook supports multiple assessment types including classwork, midterms, finals, and assignments.



Accessing the Gradebook


Before the gradebook loads, the teacher must make three selections:


  1. Select Subject: Choose from the dropdown of subjects the teacher has created
  2. Select Term: Choose the academic term (Fall Semester, Spring Semester, Winter Semester, September Term, January Term, May Term)
  3. Select Year: Use the plus/minus buttons to set the academic year

Once all three are selected, the gradebook table loads showing all enrolled students and their grades.



Entering Grades


The gradebook displays a table with students listed down the left column and grade columns across the top. Grade columns may include:


  • Classwork: Daily or weekly assignments and participation
  • Midterm Exam: Mid-semester assessment
  • Final Exam: End-of-term assessment
  • Assignments: Grades for specific assignments sent through the portal

To enter a grade, click into any cell and type the score. Grades are saved when the teacher moves to another cell or clicks outside the table. There is no separate save button — the gradebook auto-saves.



Teacher Comments


Teachers can add comments to individual student grades. To add a comment:


  1. Click the comment icon next to a student's name or grade cell
  2. A modal opens with a text area (max 400 characters)
  3. Enter the comment (e.g., "Jane has shown great improvement this semester" or "Needs to focus more on problem-solving")
  4. A character counter shows remaining space
  5. Click Save Comment

Comments are visible to students on their My Grades page and to parents on their Grades page. A comment indicator appears next to any student who has a teacher comment.



How Grades Appear to Others


  • Students: View their own grades on the My Grades page in the Student Portal. They see grades for all subjects in one view, organized by term and year. Teacher comments are viewable by clicking a comment icon.
  • Parents: View their child's grades on the Grades page in the Parent Portal, with the same term and year selectors.
  • School Admins: View any teacher's gradebook through the Gradebook Viewer in the Admin Portal (read-only).


Key Talking Points for Agents


  • "Make sure you've selected the right subject, term, and year — the gradebook won't load until all three are chosen."
  • "Grades auto-save when you move to another cell. You don't need to click a save button."
  • "Comments are limited to 400 characters and are visible to both students and parents."
  • "If you don't see a student in your gradebook, check that they're enrolled in the subject for that term."
  • "Create a new subject for each term so gradebooks stay separate — 'Biology (Fall 2026)' and 'Biology (Spring 2027)' should be different subjects."
